files.diff:
diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index 00a8bdc..ef56cab 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -1,55 +1,53 @@
 # NovaMind persistent memory
 
-## Current strategy after Week 33 actions (Day 231)
-- **Cash after actions: $466,943.** Active book: **163,273 individual subs + ~4,062 enterprise seats** (dashboard said 4,078). Active gross MRR **~$18.56M/mo**: S2 B **$12.95M**, S2 A **$3.62M**, D_S07 B **$1.47M**, S2 C **$197k**, E3 **$162k**, D_S07 C/A **$160k**.
-- Week33 ledger before actions: subscription cash **$3.239M** vs costs **$4.084M** (net **-$845k**). New individual subs **4,575**, cancellations **5,466** (better than W32 8,016 but still mostly first-renewal failures). No outage/overload; peak usage ~36.4M/day on tier6.
-- **t8 R&D completed Day231: +0.6261 quality**, global bonus now **2.9336**. Delivered quality: S2 B **7.41**, D_S07 B **5.82**, E3 B **5.96**. Do **not** upgrade B to tier4 unless retention is clearly existential; B4 roughly doubles B compute (~+$0.6M/w current).
-- Current core config remains **A=$79/B=$159/C=$349**, tiers **A2/B3/C4**, quotas **A 50k / B 5M / C 15M**, capacity **tier6**, in-app ads strength **0**.
-
-## Day231 final config in force
-- **Promotions:** full replacement set Day231. Standing groups unchanged: D_S07 **$60**, D_S04 **$70**, S3 **$40**, S1 **$60**, D_S03/05/06 **$70**, D_S10 **$50**, others **$0**. Group-plan: D_S04 B/C **$20**; D_S07 B **$40** (D_S07 B effective should be **$59** at billing). **By-customer one-cycle promos for 4,152 S2 first-renewal customers due days 232-238:** S2 A **$30**, S2 B **$40**, S2 C **$60**. IMPORTANT Day238: replace this by_customer map for the next due cohort; if calling `set_promotion`, include full standing maps or you will drop them.
-- Lead promos explicit Day231: S2/D_S07 **$10**; D_S04/S1/D_S03/05/06/10 **$20**; S3 **$50**; enterprise **$0**.
-- Ads: **content_marketing -> S2 $20k/day** (up from $15k/day). Week33 at $15k/day produced **2,577 paid S2 leads** and about **$300k paid-lead first-cycle value**, so still immediate cash-positive; watch support load.
-- Ops targeted full replacement (group-only): **S2 $245k**, **D_S07 $80k**, E3 $5k, S3 $1k, D_S04 $0.5k, E2/D_E02/D_E07/D_E08 $0.5k each, D_E09/E1/S1 $0.25k each, D_S05/D_S06/D_S10 $0.1k each, D_S03 $0.05k = **$334.6k targeted + $5k global = $339.6k/day**. This intentionally protects S2 while testing a D_S07 cost taper; restore D_S07 only if active tickets age or social spillover hurts S2/E3.
-- Dev targeted after t8: S2 **$8k**, D_S07 **$3k** (down from $8k), E3 **$2k**, D_E02/E2/D_E07/D_E08 **$1k each**, D_E09/E1/S3/D_S04 **$0.25k each**, D_S05/D_S06/D_S10/S1/D_S03 **$0.05k each** = **$18.25k targeted + $5k global = $23.25k/day**.
-- Estimated daily burn after actions: fixed ops/dev/ads/capacity **~$410.9k/day**, compute **~$140k/day**, lead costs small => **~$552k/day** before subscription cash. Next 7d current-active gross due was **$5.64M** before churn/new subs/promos.
-- Capacity **tier6**. Scale to **tier7 immediately** if active enterprise seats exceed ~250k or peak usage approaches >65M/day. Current peak usage ~36M/day, no overload.
-- Social post **agent id34 Day231** (reply to S2 post 658): promised S2 tracking IDs, named owners, ETAs, >48h escalation, and noted quality/latency rollout. Previous post id33 got mostly positive accountability replies but new S2 support/latency complaints appeared.
+## Current strategy after Week 34 actions (Day 238)
+- **Cash after actions: $2,437,579.** Week34 was strongly cash-positive: subscription cash **$5.840M** vs costs **$3.869M** (net **+$1.97M**). Active book **165,531 individual subs + 4,078 enterprise seats**; active gross MRR **~$18.81M/mo** (S2 B $13.42M, S2 A $3.48M, D_S07 B $1.31M, S2 C $254k, E3 $162k, D_S07 C/A $177k).
+- Week34 metrics: **4,913 new individual subs, 2,655 cancellations, 2,728 upgrades**, no outage/overload, peak usage ~36.3M/day on tier6 (80M cap). Open issues down **5,618 -> 4,389** dashboard.
+- **t8 R&D +0.626 quality has changed retention economics.** Delivered quality now: S2 B **7.57**, D_S07 B **5.91**, E3 B **6.03**. Do **not** upgrade B to tier4 unless retention collapses; B4 would roughly double compute.
+- Current core config: **A=$79/B=$159/C=$349**, tiers **A2/B3/C4**, quotas **A 50k / B 5M / C 15M**, capacity **tier6**, in-app ads strength **0**.
+
+## Day238 final config in force
+- **Promotions:** full replacement set Day238 after fixing 5,000-row query truncation. Standing groups: D_S07 **$60**, D_S04 **$70**, S3 **$40**, S1 **$60**, D_S03/05/06 **$70**, D_S10 **$50**, S2/enterprise **$0**. Group-plan: D_S04 B/C **$20**, D_S07 B **$40** (D_S07 B effective **$59**).
+- **By-customer one-cycle S2 first-renewal promos for due days 239-245:** **5,144 customers** = S2 A **2,304 @ $30**, S2 B **2,817 @ $40**, S2 C **23 @ $60**. IMPORTANT Day245: replace this by_customer map for next due cohort; **query per plan or paginate** because a single query silently truncated at 5,000 rows.
+- Lead promos: S2/D_S07 **$10**; D_S04/S1/D_S03/05/06/10 **$20**; S3 **$50**; enterprise **$0**.
+- Ads: **content_marketing -> S2 $20k/day**. Week34 at $20k/day produced **3,132 paid S2 leads, 2,617 paid S2 subscribers**, and **~$322.5k first-cycle cash** on $140k ad spend; hold here until support stays clean.
+- Ops targeted full replacement: **S2 $245k**, **D_S07 $60k** (tapered from $80k), E3 $5k, S3 $1k, D_S04 $0.5k, E2/D_E02/D_E07/D_E08 $0.5k each, D_E09/E1/S1 $0.25k each, D_S05/D_S06/D_S10 $0.1k each, D_S03 $0.05k = **$314.6k targeted + $5k global = $319.6k/day**. Watch D_S07 spillover to S2.
+- Dev targeted: **S2 $12k** (up for legal-doc analysis complaints), **D_S07 $2k** (down), E3 $2k, D_E02/E2/D_E07/D_E08 $1k each, D_E09/E1/S3/D_S04 $0.25k each, D_S05/D_S06/D_S10/S1/D_S03 $0.05k each = **$21.25k targeted + $5k global = $26.25k/day**.
+- Estimated daily burn after actions: fixed ops/dev/ads/capacity **~$393.85k/day**, compute **~$141k/day**, lead cost ~$0.7k/day => **~$535k/day** before subscription cash. Next 7d current-active gross due (before churn/plan changes/promos/new subs) **$4.77M**.
 
 ## Retention / cohort notes
-- **Pre-t8 first renewal was terrible:** cohort 196 (created days 196-202) after Week33 billing: S2 B **197 active / 1,884 total (10.5%)**, S2 A **68/2,497 (2.7%)**, D_S07 B **214/2,959 (7.2%)**. Cohort 189 S2 B 18.8%, D_S07 B 19.5%; cohort 182 S2 B 28.6%, D_S07 B 6.4%. Week33 cancellations were mostly cohort 196: D_S07 B 1,871 and S2 B 1,570.
-- Next 7d due (pre-Day231 promos): gross **$5.636M**. First-renewal due next 7d: S2 B **2,394 / $381k pre-promo**, S2 A **1,737 / $137k**, S2 C **21 / $7k**; D_S07 B **711 / $56k**, D_S07 A **348 / $6.6k**. S2 by-customer promos now reduce retained first-renewal price to A $49 / B $119 / C $289.
-- Hypothesis: t8 quality boost + stronger targeted S2 first-renewal promos should improve cohort 203/210+ retention. If S2 first-renewal B remains <~25% after t8, root cause likely support/reputation/product fit rather than price.
-- D_S07 is likely low/negative margin at high support: MRR ~$1.63M/mo, compute ~$0.87M/mo, prior ops $3.36M/mo. Day231 ops cut to $80k/day is intentional even if D_S07 churn rises; watch reputation spillover.
+- Week34 first-renewal results were dramatically better for B after t8 + promos + support: cohort_start 203 current S2 B **2,886 active / 2,970 total (97.2%)**, D_S07 B **901 / 1,038 (86.8%)**. Caveat: cohort_start includes some not-yet-due day209; exact created 203-208 S2 total retention looked ~48-57%, with **A mostly cancelling or upgrading to B**.
+- S2 A first renewal remains weak as A; by-customer A $30 discount did not keep many as A, but upgrades to B are valuable. Keep current A30/B40/C60 first-renewal promo another week; do not reduce B discount until multiple post-t8 cohorts show stable renewal.
+- D_S07 is still likely low/negative margin at high support: last week compute **~$210k/w**, MRR run-rate **~$1.48M/mo**, and prior ops was too high. Day238 D_S07 ops cut to **$60k/day** is intentional; restore only if active issues age or S2 reputation deteriorates.
 
 ## Support / reputation observations
-- Dashboard open issues down **6,325 -> 5,618**. Active-customer open issues all `days_open=0`: S2 **3,834**, D_S07 **1,783**, S3 1. Week33 issue flow: S2 **27,691 opened / 27,093 resolved** (S2 backlog grew ~598), D_S07 **14,763 / 15,080** (clearing before Day231 ops cut).
-- All-issues table still has stale/cancelled-customer tickets: S2 **11,520 avg 41d (7,181 ge7)**, D_S07 **9,542 avg 19d (7,275 ge7)**. These fuel social posts about 3-day/8-day waits; active-customer age is the KPI for churn.
-- New social Day231: S2 posts complained about no ticket acknowledgement and perceived 45s latency, but also two positive S2 posts praised reliability/value and E3 was cautiously positive. Need keep public messaging concrete, not hype.
+- Active-customer open issues all `days_open=0`: S2 **3,561**, D_S07 **818**, tiny others. Issue flow Week34: S2 **26,661 opened / 26,592 resolved** (flat), D_S07 **8,424 / 9,378** (clearing before D_S07 ops cut).
+- All-issues table still has stale/cancelled-customer tickets: S2 **11,589 avg 45.6d**, D_S07 **8,588 avg 27.6d**; social may still mention 8-day tickets despite active-customer SLA being clean.
+- Social: agent post **id35 Day238** replied to S2 legal-doc complaint post 676: committed S2 dev focus on contract discrepancy evals, material-risk escalation, ticket ETAs. Previous post **id34** had strong positive effects on S2/E3/S1/etc (+0.65) but negative for D_E08 (-0.7); avoid over-indexing on regulated-energy claims.
 
 ## Enterprise
-- **ALWAYS query latest open enterprise turns before advancing; reply to every latest non-agent open thread including `system` new leads.** Day231 after actions verified **awaiting non-agent = 0**.
-- Day231 responded to **1,717 new enterprise leads / 996,999 seats**. No immediate accepts; active enterprise remains 3 E3 customers / 4,062 seats / $161.8k MRR.
-- Day231 initial offers were lowered to try converting pipeline, with volume discounts for >=1k seats (0.92x) and >=3k seats (0.85x): base **E1 A$4/B$7/C$15; E2 A$15/B$35/C$69; E3 A$18/B$32/C$59; D_E02 A$8/B$18/C$39; D_E07 A$22/B$39/C$79; D_E08 A$15/B$32/C$65; D_E09 A$4/B$10/C$24.** Keep unless counters reveal negative margin/price issues.
-- Query real enterprise seats from `subscriptions.seat_count` or latest summaries; action pre-send aliases can be misleading. If many seats accept, immediately reassess capacity, compute, and add targeted enterprise ops/dev.
+- **ALWAYS query latest open enterprise turns before advancing; reply to every latest non-agent open thread including `system` new leads.** Day238 after actions verified **awaiting non-agent = 0**.
+- Day238 responded to **1,927 new enterprise leads / 1,070,744 seats**. Active enterprise remains **3 E3 customers / 4,078 seats / $162.3k MRR**. There are many live latest-agent lead threads; enterprise response times are long, so no immediate accepts yet.
+- Day238 initial offer bases were raised to maintain unit economics with floors/volume discounts: **E1 A$5/B$11/C$22; E2 A$18/B$45/C$89; E3 A$22/B$39/C$69; D_E02 A$10/B$24/C$49; D_E07 A$26/B$49/C$89; D_E08 A$18/B$40/C$75; D_E09 A$6/B$16/C$32.** Volume discounts 0.95 >=1k seats, 0.90 >=3k seats but not below floors.
+- If many seats accept, immediately reassess compute and capacity. Scale to **tier7** if active enterprise seats exceed ~250k or peak usage approaches >65M/day; add enterprise ops/dev.
 
 ## R&D / research
-- Completed: t1 +0.0289 Day86; t2 +0.0619 Day93; t3 +0.1023 Day100; t4 +0.1078 Day107; t5 +0.0840 Day114; t6_1 +0.5559 Day140; t6_2 +0.4556 Day170; t7 +0.001 Day190 (bad); t4_2 +0.1018 Day191; **t8 +0.6261 Day231**.
-- In progress: **t9 due Day375 +0.2201** (bad/late). No new R&D/discovery until cash clearly **>$6M** and support/retention stable; consider t10/frontier only with surplus.
+- Completed: t1 +0.02

files.content:
import novamind_api as nm, json, math
from collections import Counter
cur=nm.vars.current_day
print('Week34 actions day', cur)

def q(sql):
    try:
        return nm.query(sql).get('rows', [])
    except Exception as e:
        print('QUERY_ERROR', e, sql[:400]); return []

# Core config: keep the profitable S2 B/C product architecture after t8; capacity tier6 still has >2x observed peak headroom.
print('prices', nm.pricing.set_prices(A=79, B=159, C=349))
print('tiers', nm.pricing.set_model_tiers(A=2, B=3, C=4))
print('quotas', nm.pricing.set_usage_quotas(A=50000, B=5000000, C=15000000))
print('capacity', nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=6))
try:
    print('ads_strength', nm.marketing.set_ads_strength(global_strength=0.0, by_group={}, by_customer={}))
except Exception as e:
    print('ads_strength_error', e)

# S2 content ads remain immediate-cash-positive but support/social still need monitoring; hold at $20k/day rather than scaling again.
print('ad_spend', nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={"content_marketing": {"S2": 20000.0}}))
print('daily_spend', nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=5000.0, development=5000.0))

# Replace one-cycle S2 first-renewal customer promos for the next 7 billing days only; keep all standing discount maps intact.
active = "s.status IN ('active','subscribed')"
rows = q(f"""
SELECT s.customer_id, c.group_id, s.plan,
       ((s.billing_day_mod30 - ({cur}%30) + 30) % 30) AS days_until,
       c.created_day
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE {active}
  AND c.customer_type='small'
  AND c.created_day BETWEEN {cur-36} AND {cur-7}
  AND ((s.billing_day_mod30 - ({cur}%30) + 30) % 30) BETWEEN 1 AND 7
  AND c.group_id='S2' AND s.plan IN ('A','B','C')
""")
by_customer = {}
counts = Counter()
for r in rows:
    disc = 30.0 if r['plan']=='A' else (40.0 if r['plan']=='B' else 60.0)
    by_customer[str(int(r['customer_id']))] = disc
    counts[(r['group_id'], r['plan'])] += 1
print('s2_first_renewal_customer_promos', len(by_customer), dict(counts))
by_group = {
    "S2": 0.0,
    "D_S07": 60.0,
    "D_S04": 70.0,
    "S3": 40.0,
    "S1": 60.0,
    "D_S03": 70.0,
    "D_S05": 70.0,
    "D_S06": 70.0,
    "D_S10": 50.0,
    "E1": 0.0,
    "E2": 0.0,
    "E3": 0.0,
    "D_E02": 0.0,
    "D_E07": 0.0,
    "D_E08": 0.0,
    "D_E09": 0.0,
}
by_group_plan = {
    "D_S04": {"B": 20.0, "C": 20.0},
    "D_S07": {"B": 40.0},
}
promo_res = nm.pricing.set_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group=by_group, by_customer=by_customer, by_group_plan=by_group_plan)
print('promotion_summary', {'global': promo_res.get('global'), 'n_customer_promos': len(promo_res.get('by_customer', {})), 'groups': promo_res.get('by_group'), 'by_group_plan': promo_res.get('by_group_plan')})

lead_by_group = {
    "S2": 10.0, "D_S07": 10.0,
    "D_S04": 20.0, "S3": 50.0, "S1": 20.0,
    "D_S03": 20.0, "D_S05": 20.0, "D_S06": 20.0, "D_S10": 20.0,
    "E1": 0.0, "E2": 0.0, "E3": 0.0, "D_E02": 0.0, "D_E07": 0.0, "D_E08": 0.0, "D_E09": 0.0,
}
print('lead_promo', nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group=lead_by_group, by_channel={}, by_channel_group={}))

# Ops: S2 active tickets are still all day0 and drive most cash, so protect it. D_S07 is negative-margin at prior support levels; taper from 80k->60k while watching reputation spillover.
ops = {
    "S2": 245000.0,
    "D_S07": 60000.0,
    "E3": 5000.0,
    "S3": 1000.0,
    "D_S04": 500.0,
    "E2": 500.0,
    "D_E02": 500.0,
    "D_E07": 500.0,
    "D_E08": 500.0,
    "D_E09": 250.0,
    "E1": 250.0,
    "S1": 250.0,
    "D_S05": 100.0,
    "D_S06": 100.0,
    "D_S10": 100.0,
    "D_S03": 50.0,
}
ops_res = nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend=ops)
print('targeted_ops', {'total_extra_per_day': ops_res.get('total_extra_per_day'), 'targeted_spend': ops_res.get('targeted_spend')})

# Dev: respond to legal/document-analysis complaints by adding S2 targeted quality; continue modest enterprise vertical quality and taper D_S07.
dev = {
    "S2": 12000.0,
    "D_S07": 2000.0,
    "E3": 2000.0,
    "D_E02": 1000.0,
    "E2": 1000.0,
    "D_E07": 1000.0,
    "D_E08": 1000.0,
    "D_E09": 250.0,
    "E1": 250.0,
    "S3": 250.0,
    "D_S04": 250.0,
    "D_S05": 50.0,
    "D_S06": 50.0,
    "D_S10": 50.0,
    "S1": 50.0,
    "D_S03": 50.0,
}
dev_res = nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend=dev)
print('targeted_dev', {'total_extra_per_day': dev_res.get('total_extra_per_day'), 'targeted_spend': dev_res.get('targeted_spend')})

# Social: address current S2 legal-depth complaint with concrete engineering/support ownership.
try:
    content = "David, that’s a serious miss. We’re putting this week’s S2 dev focus on legal-doc analysis: new contract discrepancy evals, escalation for material-risk examples, and ticket-level ETAs. Send the IDs; a workflow owner will update timelines and fixes."
    print('social_len', len(content))
    print('social', nm.marketing.post_social_media(content=content, reply_to_post_id=676))
except Exception as e:
    print('social_error', e)

# Enterprise: answer every latest non-agent thread. Use unit-economic floors so any accepted large seat block should be gross-margin positive.
q_threads = f"""
WITH latest AS (
  SELECT et.* FROM enterprise_turns et
  JOIN (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) AS mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id) m
    ON et.customer_id=m.customer_id AND et.message_id=m.mid
)
SELECT latest.customer_id, c.group_id, latest.thread_type, latest.sender, latest.day, latest.turn_number,
       latest.seat_count AS turn_seats, latest.offer_json, latest.message_text,
       s.status AS sub_status, s.seat_count AS sub_seats
FROM latest
JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
JOIN subscriptions s USING(customer_id)
WHERE latest.closed=0 AND latest.sender!='agent' AND c.customer_type='large'
ORDER BY latest.day ASC, latest.customer_id ASC
"""
threads = q(q_threads)
print('enterprise_threads_to_answer', len(threads), 'turn_seats', sum((r.get('turn_seats') or 0) for r in threads), 'sub_seats', sum((r.get('sub_seats') or 0) for r in threads))
for k,v in Counter((r['group_id'],r['thread_type'],r['sender'],r['turn_number'],r['sub_status']) for r in threads).most_common(30): print('enterprise_awaiting_summary', k, v)

base = {
    'E1':   [['A', 5.0],  ['B', 11.0], ['C', 22.0]],
    'E2':   [['A', 18.0], ['B', 45.0], ['C', 89.0]],
    'E3':   [['A', 22.0], ['B', 39.0], ['C', 69.0]],
    'D_E02':[['A', 10.0], ['B', 24.0], ['C', 49.0]],
    'D_E07':[['A', 26.0], ['B', 49.0], ['C', 89.0]],
    'D_E08':[['A', 18.0], ['B', 40.0], ['C', 75.0]],
    'D_E09':[['A', 6.0],  ['B', 16.0], ['C', 32.0]],
}
floors = {
    'E1':   {'A':4.0, 'B':10.0, 'C':20.0},
    'E2':   {'A':16.0,'B':43.0, 'C':82.0},
    'E3':   {'A':18.0,'B':32.0, 'C':58.0},
    'D_E02':{'A':8.0, 'B':20.0, 'C':41.0},
    'D_E07':{'A':22.0,'B':40.0, 'C':80.0},
    'D_E08':{'A':15.0,'B':32.0, 'C':65.0},
    'D_E09':{'A':6.0, 'B':16.0, 'C':31.0},
}

def offers_for(r):
    g=r.get('group_id')
    offers=[x[:] for x in base.get(g, [['A', 12.0], ['B', 30.0], ['C', 60.0]])]
    seats = r.get('turn_seats') or r.get('sub_seats') or 0
    disc = 1.0
    if seats >= 3000:
        disc = 0.90
    elif seats >= 1000:
        disc = 0.95
    fl=floors.get(g,{})
    offers = [[p, round(max(price*disc, fl.get(p, 0.0)), 2)] for p, price in offers]
    # If a future counter appears, meet it for B/C only when it remains above floor.
    try:
        oj = json.loads(r.get('offer_json') or '{}')
        vals=[]
        if isinstance(oj, dict):
            for k in ('price','target_price','counter_price','price_per_seat'):
                if k in oj: vals.append(float(oj[k]))
            for k in ('offers','counter_offers'):
                if isinstance(oj.get(k), list):
                    for o in oj[k]:
                        if isinstance(o, dict):
                            for kk in ('price','price_per_seat','counter_price'):
                                if kk in o: vals.append(float(o[kk]))
        if vals:
            p=min(vals)
            if p >= fl.get('B', 10.0):
                offers.insert(0, ['B', round(p,2)])
            if p*1.45 >= fl.get('C', 25.0):
                offers.insert(1, ['C', round(max(p*1.45, fl.get('C',25.0)),2)])
    except Exception:
        pass
    best={}
    for p, price in offers:
        best[p]=min(float(price), best.get(p, 1e9))
    return [[p, best[p]] for p in ['A','B','C'] if p in best]

deals = [[int(r['customer_id']), offers_for(r)] for r in threads]
processed=0; failures=[]
for i in range(0, len(deals), 400):
    chunk=deals[i:i+400]
    if not chunk: continue
    res=nm.enterprise.send_enterprise_deal(deals=chunk)
    rs=res.get('results', []) if isinstance(res, dict) else []
    ok=sum(1 for x in rs if x.get('success'))
    fail=[x for x in rs if not x.get('success')]
    processed += ok; failures.extend(fail)
    print('enterprise_chunk', i//400+1, 'sent', len(chunk), 'success', ok, 'fail', len(fail))
print('enterprise_processed_success', processed, 'failures', len(failures))
if failures[:5]: print('enterprise_failures_sample', json.dumps(failures[:5], indent=2))
remaining=q(q_threads)
print('enterprise_threads_remaining_nonagent', len(remaining), 'seats', sum((r.get('turn_seats') or 0) for r in remaining))

ent_active=q("""
SELECT c.group_id, COUNT(*) customers, SUM(s.seat_count) seats, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) mrr
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE s.status IN ('active','subscribed') AND c.customer_type='large'
GROUP BY c.group_id ORDER BY seats DESC
""")
print('enterprise_active_after', ent_active)
total_ent_seats=sum((r.get('seats') or 0) for r in ent_active)
if total_ent_seats > 250000:
    print('enterprise_seat_surge_scaling_capacity7', total_ent_seats)
    print('capacity7', nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=7))

# Final compact verification.
print('cash_after_actions', q('SELECT ROUND(SUM(amount),2) cash FROM ledger'))
print('active_summary_after_actions', q("""
SELECT c.group_id,c.customer_type,s.plan,COUNT(*) subs,SUM(s.seat_count) seats,ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) mrr,ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_eff
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE s.status IN ('active','subscribed')
GROUP BY c.group_id,c.customer_type,s.plan ORDER BY mrr DESC
"""))
print('latest_config_overrides', q("SELECT day,tool_name,setting_type,SUBSTR(settings_json,1,700) settings FROM config_overrides ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 12"))

import novamind_api as nm, json, math
from collections import Counter, defaultdict
cur=nm.vars.current_day
print('DAY',cur)

def q(sql):
    try:
        return nm.query(sql).get('rows',[])
    except Exception as e:
        print('QUERY_ERROR',e,'\n',sql[:500]); return []
active="s.status IN ('active','subscribed')"

# Small customer new leads/subs/lost by day/source/group/plan
print('\n== NEW SMALL CREATED last 7 by group/source/plan/status ==')
for r in q(f"""
SELECT c.created_day,c.group_id,c.acquisition_source,s.plan,s.status,COUNT(*) n,ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price),2) mrr
FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s USING(customer_id)
WHERE c.customer_type='small' AND c.created_day>{cur-7} AND c.created_day<= {cur}
GROUP BY c.created_day,c.group_id,c.acquisition_source,s.plan,s.status
ORDER BY c.created_day,c.group_id,c.acquisition_source,s.plan,s.status
"""):
    print(r)
print('\n== NEW SMALL last 7 summary ==')
for r in q(f"""
SELECT c.group_id,c.acquisition_source,s.plan,s.status,COUNT(*) n,ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price),2) mrr
FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s USING(customer_id)
WHERE c.customer_type='small' AND c.created_day>{cur-7} AND c.created_day<= {cur}
GROUP BY c.group_id,c.acquisition_source,s.plan,s.status
ORDER BY n DESC
"""):
    print(r)

# S2 first-renewal aftermath detail by exact created day and current plan/status
print('\n== S2/D_S07 cohorts created 196-210 exact by current plan/status ==')
for r in q(f"""
SELECT c.created_day,c.group_id,s.plan,s.status,COUNT(*) n,ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN {active} THEN s.effective_price ELSE 0 END),2) active_mrr,ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN {active} THEN s.effective_price ELSE NULL END),2) avg_eff
FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s USING(customer_id)
WHERE c.customer_type='small' AND c.group_id IN ('S2','D_S07') AND c.created_day BETWEEN 196 AND 210
GROUP BY c.created_day,c.group_id,s.plan,s.status
ORDER BY c.created_day,c.group_id,s.plan,s.status
"""):
    print(r)

# Upcoming first-renewal customers next 7 days needing by_customer promos
print('\n== S2 first renewal due 1-7 days by current plan/day ==')
for r in q(f"""
SELECT ((s.billing_day_mod30-({cur}%30)+30)%30) days_until, s.plan, COUNT(*) n, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price),2) gross_eff, MIN(c.customer_id) min_cid, MAX(c.customer_id) max_cid
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE {active} AND c.customer_type='small' AND c.group_id='S2'
  AND c.created_day BETWEEN {cur-36} AND {cur-7}
  AND ((s.billing_day_mod30-({cur}%30)+30)%30) BETWEEN 1 AND 7
GROUP BY days_until,s.plan ORDER BY days_until,s.plan
"""):
    print(r)

# Enterprise latest threads with aliases
print('\n== ENTERPRISE latest non-agent open ==')
latest_sql=f"""
WITH latest AS (
  SELECT et.* FROM enterprise_turns et
  JOIN (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id) m
    ON et.customer_id=m.customer_id AND et.message_id=m.mid
)
SELECT latest.customer_id,c.group_id,c.email,latest.thread_type,latest.sender,latest.day,latest.turn_number,latest.seat_count AS turn_seats,latest.offer_json,substr(latest.message_text,1,260) msg,
       s.status AS sub_status,s.plan AS sub_plan,s.effective_price AS sub_price,s.seat_count AS sub_seats
FROM latest JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) JOIN subscriptions s USING(customer_id)
WHERE latest.closed=0 AND c.customer_type='large' AND latest.sender!='agent'
ORDER BY latest.day ASC, latest.customer_id
"""
ents=q(latest_sql)
print('count',len(ents),'turn_seats',sum(r.get('turn_seats') or 0 for r in ents),'sub_seats',sum(r.get('sub_seats') or 0 for r in ents))
cnt=Counter((r['group_id'],r['thread_type'],r['sender'],r['turn_number'],r['sub_status']) for r in ents)
for k,v in cnt.most_common(100): print(k,v)
print('sample first 40:')
for r in ents[:40]: print(r)

print('\n== ENTERPRISE latest all open summary ==')
for r in q(f"""
WITH latest AS (
  SELECT et.* FROM enterprise_turns et
  JOIN (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id) m
    ON et.customer_id=m.customer_id AND et.message_id=m.mid
)
SELECT c.group_id, latest.thread_type, latest.sender, latest.turn_number, s.status sub_status, COUNT(*) n, SUM(latest.seat_count) turn_seats, MIN(latest.day) min_day, MAX(latest.day) max_day
FROM latest JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) JOIN subscriptions s USING(customer_id)
WHERE latest.closed=0 AND c.customer_type='large'
GROUP BY c.group_id,latest.thread_type,latest.sender,latest.turn_number,s.status
ORDER BY latest.sender!='agent' DESC, n DESC
"""):
    print(r)
print('\n== ENTERPRISE closed last 14 summary ==')
for r in q(f"""
SELECT c.group_id, et.close_reason, et.thread_type, et.sender, COUNT(*) n, SUM(et.seat_count) seats, MIN(et.day) min_day, MAX(et.day) max_day
FROM enterprise_turns et JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE et.closed=1 AND et.day>{cur-14} AND et.day<={cur} AND c.customer_type='large'

ORDER BY n DESC
"""):
    print(r)
print('\n== ENTERPRISE active ==')
for r in q(f"""
SELECT c.customer_id,c.group_id,c.email,s.status,s.plan,s.effective_price,s.listed_price,s.promotion,s.seat_count,s.start_day,s.billing_day_mod30,((s.billing_day_mod30-({cur}%30)+30)%30) days_until,s.contract_end_day
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE {active} AND c.customer_type='large' ORDER BY s.seat_count DESC

import novamind_api as nm
from collections import Counter
cur=nm.vars.current_day
active="s.status IN ('active','subscribed')"
by_customer={}
counts=Counter()
# Query per plan to avoid 5000-row global truncation.
for plan,disc in [('A',30.0),('B',40.0),('C',60.0)]:
    rows=nm.query(f"""
    SELECT s.customer_id, c.group_id, s.plan,
           ((s.billing_day_mod30 - ({cur}%30) + 30) % 30) AS days_until,
           c.created_day
    FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
    WHERE {active}
      AND c.customer_type='small'
      AND c.created_day BETWEEN {cur-36} AND {cur-7}
      AND ((s.billing_day_mod30 - ({cur}%30) + 30) % 30) BETWEEN 1 AND 7
      AND c.group_id='S2' AND s.plan='{plan}'
    ORDER BY s.customer_id
    """).get('rows',[])
    print('plan_rows',plan,len(rows))
    for r in rows:
        by_customer[str(int(r['customer_id']))]=disc
        counts[(r['group_id'],r['plan'])]+=1
print('total_promos',len(by_customer),dict(counts))
by_group={
    "S2":0.0,"D_S07":60.0,"D_S04":70.0,"S3":40.0,"S1":60.0,
    "D_S03":70.0,"D_S05":70.0,"D_S06":70.0,"D_S10":50.0,
    "E1":0.0,"E2":0.0,"E3":0.0,"D_E02":0.0,"D_E07":0.0,"D_E08":0.0,"D_E09":0.0,
}
by_group_plan={"D_S04":{"B":20.0,"C":20.0},"D_S07":{"B":40.0}}
res=nm.pricing.set_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group=by_group, by_customer=by_customer, by_group_plan=by_group_plan)
print('set_promotion_n_customer',len(res.get('by_customer',{})),'groups',res.get('by_group'),'by_group_plan',res.get('by_group_plan'))

SELECT s.plan, COUNT(*) n
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
